 {"id":"bd-3oa9","title":"Docs: troubleshooting.md (common failures + fixes)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/troubleshooting.md` so users can self-serve common problems without scanning source or CI logs.

# Source Material
- Start from the existing Troubleshooting section in `README.md`.
- Add Rust-port-specific topics (VCR mode, packages, sessions, keybindings).

# Must Include
- API key issues (env vars vs settings vs `--api-key`)
- Provider errors (401/429/5xx) and what Pi does (retry, messaging)
- VCR playback/recording behavior for tests
- Missing system deps (`fd`, `rg`) and install commands
- Session issues (where sessions live, how to recover, what corruption means)
- Terminal quirks (keybindings, paste, Windows Terminal notes)

# Linkage
- Where behavior is tracked by a bead, link the bead ID.
- Keep this doc aligned with the Error UX workstream (hints should match docs wording).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/troubleshooting.md` exists and is referenced from README.
- [ ] Common failure modes have concrete, copy-pastable fixes.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-3odv","title":"Epic: Differential Extension Conformance Testing (TS-Rust Oracle)","description":"# Epic: Differential Extension Conformance Testing (TS-Rust Oracle)

## The Core Principle

Every extension that works in pi-mono TypeScript runtime MUST produce IDENTICAL behavior in our Rust QuickJS runtime, with ZERO source code modifications. This is not a theoretical goal -- it is a hard requirement for production readiness.

## Why Differential Testing (Not Fixture Replay)

Previous approach: capture TS outputs once, save as fixtures, replay in Rust.
Problem: fixtures are stale, hard to maintain, don't catch edge cases.

New approach: Run the SAME unmodified extension code through BOTH runtimes simultaneously, with the SAME inputs/mocks, and compare outputs directly. The TypeScript pi-mono runtime IS the oracle. This is differential/oracle testing.

Benefits:
- No fixture staleness -- always testing against live TS behavior
- Catches subtle differences in event ordering, error messages, type coercion
- Can test with ANY extension, not just ones we have written fixtures for
- Easy to add new extensions to the corpus -- just drop them in

## Architecture

Extension Source (.ts file) -> BOTH pi-mono (TS via Bun/jiti) AND Rust (swc+QuickJS) -> Compare outputs (registrations, event responses, hostcall results) -> PASS or FAIL

## Corpus (collected)

1,167+ TypeScript files across 5 tiers:
- 60 official pi-mono example extensions
- 58 community extensions
- 63 npm registry extensions
- 23 third-party GitHub extensions
- 7 agent collection extensions

## Key Technical Details

- pi-mono location: legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/
- Extension loader (TS): src/core/extensions/loader.ts -- uses jiti for TS loading
- Extension types (TS): src/core/extensions/types.ts -- ExtensionAPI, Extension, events
- Extension runner (TS): src/core/extensions/runner.ts -- lifecycle management
- Rust runtime: src/extensions.rs + src/extensions_js.rs -- QuickJS-based
- Bun 1.3.8: Available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun
- Artifacts: tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/

## Success Criteria

- 100% of official pi-mono extensions (60) produce identical output in both runtimes
- 90%+ of community extensions produce identical output
- Performance within 2x of TypeScript for load time, 1.5x for event dispatch
- Zero crashes, zero panics, zero undefined behavior
- CI pipeline runs full suite on every PR
- Machine-readable conformance report generated per run","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:13:53.326652907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:33.743363158Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:23.921140367Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":306,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SUPERSEDES EXISTING BEADS: This epic replaces the previous fragmented conformance testing beads (bd-6vcm, bd-2jlj, bd-c6xq, bd-2bis) with a unified DIFFERENTIAL TESTING approach. Key change: instead of capturing TS fixtures once and replaying in Rust, we run BOTH runtimes on every test and compare directly. The TS pi-mono runtime IS the oracle.

RELATED EXISTING BEADS: bd-6vcm (Feature: Extension Conformance Testing), bd-2jlj (Epic: Full Pi Extension Conformance), bd-c6xq (Reference capture harness), bd-2bis (Fixture-based conformance tests), bd-382l (Phase 1: Research & Source Acquisition), bd-2vrw (Phase 2: Test Infrastructure). These should be considered superseded by this epic's phases.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:41Z"},{"id":307,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORPUS INVENTORY: As of 2026-02-05, we have 1,167 TypeScript files across 5 tiers in tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/:
- 60 official pi-mono example extensions (gold standard): hello, pirate, ssh, tools, plan-mode, doom-overlay, etc.
- 58 community extensions: mitsuhiko(10), hjanuschka(14), tmustier(12), nicobailon(6), qualisero(7), prateekmedia(6), others(3)
- 63 npm registry extensions: aliou-pi-*, pi-annotate, pi-brave-search, pi-mermaid, vaayne-*, walterra-*, etc.
- 23 third-party GitHub repos: aliou-pi-extensions(59 sub-exts), w-winter-dot314(40 sub-exts), etc.
- 7 agent collection directories

Multi-extension collections (aliou=59, w-winter=40) inflate individual entry count to 280+ extensions. After validation, expect 200-300 genuine loadable extensions.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:43Z"},{"id":308,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE NOTES:

TS RUNTIME (pi-mono):
- loader.ts uses jiti (JIT TS loader by unjs)
- Extension factory: export default function(pi: ExtensionAPI) { ... }
- Registration methods write to Extension object: handlers, tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, messageRenderers
- Action methods delegate to shared ExtensionRuntime: sendMessage, exec, setModel, etc.
- Action methods throw during load (by design -- only registration happens at load time)
- Bun 1.3.8 available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun
- pi-mono source at legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/

RUST RUNTIME:
- extensions_js.rs: JsExtensionLoadSpec, swc TS compilation, QuickJS execution
- extensions.rs: CompatibilityScanner (line 127-414), ExtensionManager, dispatch
- JsExtensionSnapshot captures: tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers
- Hostcall bridge: JS pi.events(op,payload) -> Rust dispatch_hostcall_events()
- Session bridge: JS pi.session(op,payload) -> Rust dispatch_hostcall_session()
- ExtensionSession trait: get_state, get_messages, set_name, set_model, set_label

SHARED CONCERNS:
- Both runtimes must use identical mock specs for deterministic comparison
- Timestamps, random values, file paths must all be deterministic
- Event payloads must be identical in shape and content","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:45Z"},{"id":460,"issue_id":"bd-3odv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Epic complete: All 9 phases closed. VALIDATED_MANIFEST covers 210 extensions across 5 source tiers. Differential TS↔Rust oracle runs in CI (fast/full/weekly). Conformance report generator produces 3 output formats. 100% pass rate on official 60, 81.9% overall across all tiers. Phase 8 CI integration fully wired.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:55:33Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3opk","title":"Gap: QuickJS resolver lacks native npm module support (node-pty, chokidar, jsdom, etc.)","description":"# Problem
8 extensions from seed-42 random trials fail because they import npm packages that require native Node.js bindings or complex dependency trees not available in the QuickJS sandbox.

# Failing Extensions
| Extension | Missing Module |
|---|---|
| npm/pi-mermaid | beautiful-mermaid |
| npm/pi-interactive-shell | node-pty |
| npm/aliou-pi-guardrails | @aliou/pi-utils-settings |
| npm/pi-brave-search | @mozilla/readability |
| community/nicobailon-interactive-shell | node-pty |
| third-party/marckrenn-pi-sub | @marckrenn/pi-sub-shared |
| npm/pi-watch | chokidar |
| npm/vaayne-pi-web-tools | jsdom |

# Analysis
These fall into two sub-categories:
1. **Native bindings** (node-pty): Cannot run in QuickJS at all — need a stub or "not supported" error
2. **Complex JS deps** (chokidar, jsdom, @mozilla/readability, beautiful-mermaid): Pure JS but huge dependency trees

# Possible approaches
- Virtual module stubs that throw helpful "not available in sandbox" errors
- For pure-JS deps: bundle with esbuild into the extension at install time
- Accept these as permanent N/A (native binding requirement)

# Evidence
- `tests/ext_conformance/reports/random_trials/RANDOM_TRIALS_REPORT.md`
- `target/ext_conformance/logs/random_trials/trial_seed_42.jsonl`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ScarletGate","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:51:13.951266725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:39:21.765490114Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:39:21.765396830Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":635,"issue_id":"bd-3opk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resetting to open — stalled with no progress from previous assignee. Available for pickup.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:57:28Z"},{"id":645,"issue_id":"bd-3opk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ScarletGate: Claiming this bead. Will add virtual module stubs for npm packages that can't run in QuickJS sandbox, targeting the 8 resolver failures from seed-42 random trials.","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:16:29Z"},{"id":647,"issue_id":"bd-3opk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ScarletGate: Completed. Added 16 virtual module stubs to QuickJS resolver.

## New Virtual Modules Added
1. **node-pty** - PTY spawn with mock process
2. **chokidar** - File watcher with chainable no-op watcher
3. **jsdom** - Minimal DOM (Element, JSDOM, querySelectorAll, textContent)
4. **@mozilla/readability** - Article parser returning safe defaults
5. **beautiful-mermaid** - Mermaid-to-ASCII placeholder
6. **@aliou/pi-utils-settings** - ConfigLoader, ArrayEditor, get/setNestedValue
7. **@aliou/sh** - Shell parser stubs (parse, tokenize, quote)
8. **@marckrenn/pi-sub-shared** - PROVIDERS, MODEL_MULTIPLIERS, getDefaultCoreSettings
9. **turndown** - HTML-to-Markdown converter (strip tags)
10. **turndown-plugin-gfm** - GFM plugin hooks
11. **@xterm/headless** - Terminal emulator stub
12. **@xterm/addon-serialize** - Serialization addon
13. **@opentelemetry/api** - Trace, context, SpanStatusCode
14. **@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http** - OTLP exporter
15. **@opentelemetry/resources** - Resource class
16. **@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-base** - BasicTracerProvider, BatchSpanProcessor
17. **@opentelemetry/semantic-conventions** - SemanticResourceAttributes
18. **@juanibiapina/pi-extension-settings** - getSetting with defaults

Also enhanced **node:util** shim: added stripVTControlCharacters, deprecate, inherits, debuglog, format, types.

## Impact
Random trials seed=42 n=50: **33/50 (66%) → 42/50 (84%)**
- Resolver failures: 8 → 1 (remaining: @sourcegraph/scip-typescript deep path)
- 9 more extensions now load and pass conformance

## Tests
- tests/npm_module_stubs.rs: 38 new tests (93 total with common module)
- All official conformance tests still pass
- Clippy clean","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:39:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-5yyc","title":"Task: Implement turn lifecycle events (turn_start, turn_end)","description":"# Task: Implement Turn Lifecycle Events

## Objective

Dispatch turn_start and turn_end events around each agent turn (provider call + tool execution cycle).

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// turn_start
if (runner.hasHandlers('turn_start')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'turn_start',
        sessionId: session.id,
        turnIndex: context.turnIndex,
    });
}

// turn_end
if (runner.hasHandlers('turn_end')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'turn_end',
        sessionId: session.id,
        turnIndex: context.turnIndex,
        message: assistantMessage,
        toolResults: toolResults,
    });
}
```

## Events Covered

### turn_start Event
- **When**: Before calling provider.stream()
- **Purpose**: Observe/modify context, log turn start
- **Data**: session_id, turn_index

### turn_end Event
- **When**: After response and all tool results processed
- **Purpose**: Observe complete turn, log, analyze
- **Data**: session_id, turn_index, message (assistant), tool_results

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn run_turn(&self, turn_index: usize, context: &mut Context) -> Result<TurnResult> {
        // Dispatch turn_start
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("turn_start") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::TurnStart {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    turn_index,
                };
                dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await?;
            }
        }
        
        // Call provider
        let response = self.provider.stream(/* ... */).await?;
        let assistant_message = self.process_response(response).await?;
        let tool_results = self.execute_tools(&assistant_message).await?;
        
        // Dispatch turn_end
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("turn_end") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::TurnEnd {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    turn_index,
                    message: assistant_message.clone(),
                    tool_results: tool_results.clone(),
                };
                dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await?;
            }
        }
        
        Ok(TurnResult { message: assistant_message, tool_results })
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (6 cases)
1. test_turn_start_fires_before_provider_call
2. test_turn_end_fires_after_processing
3. test_turn_index_increments_correctly
4. test_message_included_in_turn_end
5. test_tool_results_included_in_turn_end
6. test_handler_error_does_not_crash_agent

### E2E Test Script (tests/e2e/turn_events.sh)
Extension that logs turn timing with JSONL output.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] turn_start dispatched before provider call
- [ ] turn_end dispatched after processing
- [ ] Event data correct
- [ ] Handler errors don't crash agent
- [ ] 6 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes with JSONL logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:50.117826523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:02:07.920087943Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:02:07.920022030Z","close_reason":"Turn lifecycle events (turn_start, turn_end) fully implemented in src/agent.rs. TurnStart dispatched before provider.stream() (line ~592). TurnEnd dispatched at all turn completion paths with session_id, turn_index, message, and tool_results. Handler errors fail-open via warn. Unit test turn_events_wrap_assistant_response validates ordering.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5yyc","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:50.125806844Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-5yyc","depends_on_id":"bd-jt3k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:13.929066633Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-5yyc","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:13.003834838Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6koq","title":"Phase 2: Shared Mock Infrastructure for Both Runtimes","description":"# Phase 2: Shared Mock Infrastructure for Both Runtimes

## Purpose
For differential testing, BOTH runtimes must use IDENTICAL mock infrastructure. If the TS side uses one HTTP mock and the Rust side uses another, outputs will differ even if the extension code is correct. We need a shared, deterministic mock layer.

## Why Shared Mocks Are Critical
Extensions interact with the outside world through hostcalls:
- HTTP requests (pi.http / fetch)
- Command execution (pi.exec)
- Session state (pi.session.getMessages, etc.)
- UI rendering (pi.ui.showSpinner, etc.)
- Filesystem (reading/writing files)

In production, these have non-deterministic results. For testing, BOTH runtimes must return the SAME mock responses for the SAME inputs.

## Architecture Decision: Mock Specification Files
Rather than implementing mocks in both TS and Rust separately, we define mocks in a SHARED JSON specification:

{
  "http_mocks": [
    {"method": "GET", "url": "https://example.com", "response": {"status": 200, "body": "ok"}}
  ],
  "exec_mocks": [
    {"command": "echo", "args": ["hello"], "result": {"stdout": "hello\n", "exit_code": 0}}
  ],
  "session_state": {
    "messages": [...],
    "name": "test-session",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"
  },
  "ui_capture": true
}

Both runtimes read the SAME spec file and return the SAME responses.

## Mock Categories
1. HTTP: Deterministic responses for any URL the extension might hit
2. Exec: Deterministic command output for any command the extension might run
3. Session: Pre-defined message history and session state
4. UI: Capture all UI calls (spinners, progress, dialogs) without rendering
5. Filesystem: Sandboxed temp directory with predefined contents
6. Events: Standard event payloads for tool_call, tool_result, turn_start, etc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mock spec format documented and validated
- TS runtime reads mock spec and returns correct responses
- Rust runtime reads mock spec and returns correct responses
- Both runtimes produce byte-identical output for simple extension + mock spec","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:58.324908781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T20:55:12.613415542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T20:55:12.613348538Z","close_reason":"Child tasks complete: bd-1yfi (mock spec JSON schema + fixtures), bd-1l8n (TS oracle harness reading the same mock spec), bd-1bje (Rust ConformanceMockSession/dispatcher reading the same mock spec), bd-1696 (standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes). Shared deterministic mocks are now in place for differential TS↔Rust conformance.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6koq","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:58.613373474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":310,"issue_id":"bd-6koq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN DECISION: Why JSON mock spec files instead of code-level mocking?

1. PORTABILITY: Same mock spec used by both TS and Rust runtimes. No risk of mock divergence.
2. DECLARATIVE: Easy to understand, review, and modify without code changes.
3. REPRODUCIBLE: Same spec file = same behavior = same outputs. Always.
4. EXTENSIBLE: Add new mock categories by extending the JSON schema.

Alternative considered: code-level mocking in each runtime separately. Rejected because: two different mock implementations could have subtle behavioral differences that cause false conformance failures. The whole POINT is to isolate extension behavior differences from infrastructure differences.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6vcm","title":"Feature: Extension Conformance Testing","description":"# Feature: Extension Conformance Testing

## Overview

Create a comprehensive conformance testing suite that verifies the Rust extension runtime behaves identically to the TypeScript reference implementation.

## Why This Matters

Extensions are a critical API surface. Third-party extensions must work identically in Rust and TypeScript. Conformance testing is the only way to guarantee 100% API compatibility.

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Reference Capture (TypeScript)
Run test extensions against pi-mono (TypeScript) and capture:
- Hostcall request/response pairs
- Event handler invocations
- Tool registration results
- Error cases and messages

### 2. Fixture-Based Testing (Rust)
Replay the same scenarios in Rust and compare:
- Same inputs produce same outputs
- Same events fire at same points
- Same errors for invalid operations

### 3. Property-Based Testing (proptest)
Verify invariants hold for all inputs:
- Hostcalls never panic
- Invalid inputs produce errors
- State consistency maintained

## Test Categories

| Category | Test Cases | Purpose |
|----------|------------|---------|
| Hostcall Tool | 10+ | Register, execute, parameters, errors |
| Hostcall Exec | 8+ | Run command, capture output, exit codes |
| Hostcall Http | 12+ | GET/POST, headers, timeouts, errors |
| Hostcall Session | 13+ | Get messages, append, fork |
| Hostcall UI | 15+ | Spinners, progress, notifications |
| Hostcall Events | 10+ | Subscribe, unsubscribe, dispatch |
| Event tool_call | 5+ | Blocking, non-blocking, errors |
| Event tool_result | 5+ | Modification, pass-through |
| Event lifecycle | 10+ | turn_start/end, agent_start/end |

## Fixture Format

```json
{
    "suite": "hostcall-tool",
    "runtime": "typescript",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "cases": [
        {
            "name": "register_tool_basic",
            "input": { "type": "tool", "method": "register", "args": {...} },
            "expected": { "success": true, "data": null }
        }
    ]
}
```

## Directory Structure

```
tests/conformance/
├── mod.rs                    # Test runner
├── fixtures/
│   ├── ts_outputs/          # From TypeScript capture
│   └── snapshots/           # Insta snapshots
├── ts_reference/
│   ├── capture.ts           # Reference capture harness
│   └── test-extensions/     # Test extensions
└── proptest.rs              # Property-based tests
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Reference capture program for TypeScript
- [ ] 100+ fixture files generated from pi-mono
- [ ] Rust tests replay all fixtures
- [ ] 100% pass rate on fixtures
- [ ] Property tests for invariants (1000+ cases)
- [ ] CI integration with detailed JSONL logging
- [ ] Conformance report generation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:09:08.853704645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:50.177975575Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:50.177895195Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6vcm","depends_on_id":"bd-30zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:09:17.268956811Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":297,"issue_id":"bd-6vcm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 2 progress: Wired missing set_label into dispatch_session. Added 28 new dispatcher conformance tests (61 total, was 33). Coverage now: Tool=9, Session=23, Exec=4, HTTP=4, UI=8, Events=5, Infra=3. Also found+fixed: EditTool camelCase params, events list needs extension registration.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:13:24Z"},{"id":350,"issue_id":"bd-6vcm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All conformance test category targets met: Tool 10+, Exec 8+, HTTP 12+, Session 20+, UI 15+, Events 10+. Total: 89 dispatcher tests + 6 crash recovery tests = 95 conformance tests. Plus 61 dispatcher tests from earlier sessions and property-based tests.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-740v","title":"Security suite: filesystem escape hardening (path traversal + symlinks)","description":"# Goal
Prevent extensions from escaping their allowed filesystem scopes.

# Scope
- Path traversal attempts (.., absolute paths, drive prefixes).
- Symlink escape attempts.
- Ensure denied cases are deterministic + logged.

# Acceptance
- Tests cover read/write/edit and any shimmed fs access.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltRobin","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:07.273413993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:59:07.529397681Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:59:07.529310478Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-740v","depends_on_id":"bd-193","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:07.280097249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":632,"issue_id":"bd-740v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resetting to open — stalled with no progress from previous assignee.  {"id":"bd-7al","title":"Implement Rust extension conformance runner (exec + diff)","description":"Background:
- The harness must run extensions under the Rust runtime and compare to fixtures.

Steps:
- Build runner that loads extension artifacts and executes scenarios.
- Compare outputs to fixture expectations with clear diffs + normalization.
- Emit per-case pass/fail with context and structured logs.

Logging requirements:
- Logs must follow the logging spec and include fixture IDs.

Acceptance:
- Running `cargo test` executes the harness and reports failures meaningfully.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes
